mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) against rate changes.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.2 percentage points month-over-month
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in over 55% of instances where the unemployment rate crosses the 0.2pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving corporate revenue growth and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative average forward return over 60 days, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.05) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side resilience, generating positive short-term S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are negative in more than 60% of periods where industrial production accelerates during tightening cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index growth surpassing 3.5 percent annually forces expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in over 50% of months where CPI growth exceeds the 3.5 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market expansion, boosting consumer spending expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal fails to predict positive returns with a Rank IC above 0.04 over any 24-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Positive quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects sustained economic expansion and corporate earnings growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns show no positive correlation with GDP growth, or the signal produces negative returns in over 55% of quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales growth exceeding 0.5 percent month-over-month signals strong consumer spending momentum, immediately boosting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The threshold signal yields negative 5-day forward returns in more than 60% of triggered instances.
